signals.idea: Fix worker zombie bug: when process_single_task throws an unhandled exception (not FatalWorkerError/KeyboardInterrupt), the main loop in server.py exits, task_queue.stop() runs in finally, but the heartbeat thread keeps running — creating a zombie worker that looks active but processes nothing. The immediate trigger was a VLM import crash 22ms into loading on a cold GPU, but any unhandled exception in the task processing path causes the same zombie. Root cause is server.py:398-502 only catches FatalWorkerError and KeyboardInterrupt. Need: (1) generic exception handler in the main loop that fails the task and continues, (2) ensure the heartbeat/worker status is marked terminated when the main loop exits, (3) investigate why VLM loading crashes on cold GPU (21ms failure suggests import error not CUDA timeout). Key file: reigh-worker/source/runtime/worker/server.py

unresolved_flags.concern: Fatal error handling: Step 1's proposed inner `except Exception` would also catch `FatalWorkerError`, which would suppress the existing fatal-worker shutdown path instead of letting the worker exit.
Retry policy: Step 1's sample handler marks the task failed immediately, which bypasses the repository's existing retryable-error path for transient failures.
Heartbeat shutdown: Step 2's one-off `status="terminated"` call can race with the still-running guardian, which continues sending `status="active"` until it is forcibly killed.

VLM diagnostics: Step 4 does not actually investigate the cold-GPU import crash the brief calls out; it mostly adds more logging around code paths that already catch broad VLM import/runtime failures.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 7.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 4.

items: An unhandled exception in process_single_task no longer kills the main loop — the task is marked Failed and the worker continues polling
When the main loop exits for any reason (FatalWorkerError, KeyboardInterrupt, or unexpected), a 'terminated' heartbeat is sent and the guardian process is stopped
No zombie workers appear in the monitoring dashboard after a task processing crash
Existing tests pass without modification (or are updated if they assert on the old behavior)
New tests cover both the generic exception handler and the terminated-heartbeat-on-exit paths

unresolved_flags.concern: Fatal error handling: Step 1's proposed inner `except Exception` would also catch `FatalWorkerError`, which would suppress the existing fatal-worker shutdown path instead of letting the worker exit.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `FatalWorkerError` is an `Exception` subclass in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/task_handlers/worker/fatal_error_handler.py:22`, and `server.py` currently relies on the outer `except FatalWorkerError` at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/runtime/worker/server.py:495-497`. An inner blanket `except Exception` around `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/runtime/worker/server.py:410-493` would intercept it first unless the plan explicitly re-raises it.

unresolved_flags.concern: Retry policy: Step 1's sample handler marks the task failed immediately, which bypasses the repository's existing retryable-error path for transient failures.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current main loop routes failed task results through `is_retryable_error` and `requeue_task_for_retry` before permanently failing the task at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/runtime/worker/server.py:472-488`. The plan's handler calls `_update_task_complete(..., STATUS_FAILED, ...)` directly, so exceptions matching retryable patterns would stop retrying.

unresolved_flags.concern: Heartbeat shutdown: Step 2's one-off `status="terminated"` call can race with the still-running guardian, which continues sending `status="active"` until it is forcibly killed.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `guardian_main` loops forever and unconditionally sends active heartbeats every 20 seconds until process death at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/runtime/worker/guardian.py:130-137`. The plan proposes sending a final terminated heartbeat from `server.py` and only then terminating the guardian, but there is no stop signal or handshake to prevent a later active heartbeat from overwriting that terminal state.

unresolved_flags.concern: VLM diagnostics: Step 4 does not actually investigate the cold-GPU import crash the brief calls out; it mostly adds more logging around code paths that already catch broad VLM import/runtime failures.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The existing VLM entry points already catch `RuntimeError`/`ImportError` and fall back in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/media/vlm/single_image_prompts.py:30-105` and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker/source/media/vlm/transition_prompts.py:31-45,77-97`. Adding another try/except or a docs note in `process_single_task` does not identify which import or dependency is actually failing 21ms into VLM startup.
